title: Artemis Program Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: Artemis II's SLS/Orion completed rollout to Launch Complex 39B on March 20 and remains on track for a no-earlier-than April 1 launch at 6:24 p.m. EDT. In a major policy shift, NASA Administrator Isaacman hosted the 'Ignition' event on March 24, formally cancelling the Lunar Gateway and announcing a $20 billion, 7-year program to build a permanent lunar surface base targeting sustained human presence after 2032. The Artemis II crew — Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen — entered pre-launch quarantine and are flying to Kennedy Space Center on March 27 to complete final countdown preparations. Claims and political sections have been updated to reflect the Gateway cancellation and Isaacman's revised architecture framing.
